summary: Frontend-only verification completed on http://127.0.0.1:3456 for compare-mode responsiveness and Home regression checks. Sticky CTA opens compare mode correctly on desktop/tablet, compare cards adapt layout (desktop 3-up, tablet 2+1), and mobile vendor navigation still works.

issues: HomeStickyCta button has no explicit minHeight and may render below 44px touch target on some devices.
Compare card primary action button uses minHeight: 40 (below recommended 44px mobile touch target).
Compare close button lacks explicit 44px minimum touch target sizing.

action_items: Set explicit minHeight >= 44 for sticky CTA and compare-mode action/close buttons to meet mobile tap-target guidance.
Re-run quick tablet/mobile visual pass after touch-target sizing updates to ensure no spacing regressions.
critical_code_review_comments: HomeStickyCta and compare CTA controls are missing consistent minimum touch-area sizing; this is an accessibility/usability gap for mobile-first interaction.
HomeVendorSection `sectionTitle` does not define explicit lineHeight, which can produce less predictable tablet typography rhythm across browsers.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated on desktop (1920x1080), tablet (768x1024), and mobile (390x844). Sticky CTA compare flow and vendor navigation regression passed. n8n availability and booking submission webhooks are MOCKED and not validated in this run.
rca_of_issue: Reproduction for touch-target issue: inspect `HomeStickyCta.tsx` and `HomeCompareSection.tsx`; controls rely on padding/minHeight 40 or less with no explicit 44px minimum. Mitigation: enforce minHeight >= 44 and adequate horizontal padding for all tappable compare controls.
